title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Go Client Systems · Go Retrieval
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for deterministic tests, fault injection, contract evidence, and observability, producing fault-injected client verification harness. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http fault-injected client verification harness after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through predic…
objectives: Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Fake Transport Fixtures
steps.instruction:
Order the Fake Transport Fixtures Go HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Fake Transport Fixtures.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Fake Transport Fixtures assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Fake Transport Fixtures, keep Fake Transport Fixtures connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: Go security handoff client test G-3 injects a delayed header, truncated gzip body, malformed JSON, cross-origin redirect, and cancellation. Assert the outgoing request, cleanup, failure taxonomy, attempt count, low-cardinality telemetry, and no secret leakage. Competency probe: Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

content.text: Order work so Fake Transport Fixtures has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
